channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Provides $300 Million to Support Uganda and Neighboring Countries Against Ebola
item.description: The United States has pledged $300 million to help Uganda and nearby countries respond to the growing Ebola outbreak. The money will support medical response efforts, build health infrastructure, and ...

item.title: Four-time Stanley Cup champion Claude Lemieux dies at 60
item.description: Claude Lemieux, a four-time Stanley Cup champion and one of the NHL’s most recognisable gritty forwards, has died at the age of 60, according to the NHL Alumni Association. Born in Buckingham, Quebec,...

item.title: Students for Trump co-founder arrested in Washington, D.C. on domestic violence and assault charges
item.description: Ryan Fournier, co-founder and chairman of Students for Trump, was arrested early Tuesday in Washington, D.C., facing charges of simple assault and attempted threats to cause bodily harm in connection ...

item.title: US Provides Additional $80 Million Aid To Support Ebola Response In DR Congo And Uganda
item.description: The United States don announce say e go give extra $80 million to help fight the Ebola outbreak wey dey affect Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda. This new funding bring total US support si...

item.title: Vice President Vance warns Air Force Academy graduates against heckling during commencement speech
item.description: Vice President JD Vance delivered the commencement address at the United States Air Force Academy in Colorado Springs during the 68th graduating ceremony, where more than 900 cadets were commissioned ...

item.title: Mama Jimmy and Baba Jimmy mourn loss of unborn child in US and seek protection for their children
item.description: Mama Jimmy and Baba Jimmy, who are known content creators and gospel-related influencers, are going through a very painful moment after losing their unborn child while living in the United States. The...

item.title: Widespread water outages in Kent spark anger over South East Water’s response during heatwave
item.description: Thousands of residents in Kent have been affected by significant water supply disruptions during a period of extreme heat, prompting widespread frustration and criticism of South East Water. According...

item.title: Stakeholders propose volunteer cyber corps to strengthen defenses against telecom and cyber threats
item.description: Several groups have called for the creation of a volunteer corps aimed at strengthening the Philippines’ defences against growing cyber threats, particularly those affecting telecommunications infrast...

item.title: Meta introduces monthly subscription plans for Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp with premium features
item.description: Meta don officially introduce new monthly subscription plans for Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp as dem try expand their income beyond just advertising. The new plans, named Instagram Plus, Facebook...

item.title: Lewis expands to 976 stores as it targets 1,000-store milestone following strong growth year
item.description: Retail group Lewis has reported a significant expansion in its store network, moving closer to the 1,000-store milestone after a record-breaking year of growth. The company opened 58 new stores during...

item.title: DepEd highlights impact of Philippines–South Korea teacher exchange programme on classroom innovation
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has highlighted the positive impact of the Korea-Philippines Teacher Exchange Programme (KPTEP), saying it has helped bring innovative teaching practices to Filipin...

item.title: NSW complete women’s State of Origin series sweep with 12–4 win over Queensland
item.description: New South Wales have secured a historic clean sweep in the women’s State of Origin series after defeating Queensland 12–4 in the final match on the Gold Coast. The victory capped off a dominant series...

item.title: Melanie Collins’ Career Journey from College Sports to CBS NFL Sideline Reporter
item.description: Melanie Collins, born on 6th February 1986 in State College, Pennsylvania, has steadily built a successful career in sports broadcasting. She started her media journey during her college years at Penn...

item.title: Addo xenophobic violence remains unresolved as anti-immigrant tensions resurface a year later
item.description: A year after violent xenophobic attacks in Addo, Eastern Cape, tensions between local residents and immigrant communities have resurfaced, with no arrests yet made in connection with the killings. The...

item.title: Siddaramaiah's exit and the political impact of Karnataka caste survey on Shivakumar and Rahul Gandhi
item.description: The article reports on the political developments in Karnataka following Chief Minister Siddaramaiah’s decision to step down, and the implications of his actions just before his exit. A key point high...

item.title: Tanzania Works to Restore US and UK Consular Offices in Zanzibar to Boost Trade and Tourism
item.description: The Tanzanian government is stepping up efforts to convince the United States and United Kingdom to reopen their consular offices in Zanzibar. Ambassador Mahmoud Thabit Kombo, Minister for Foreign Aff...

item.title: Heineken to host premium Champions League final viewing event in Lagos for Arsenal vs PSG
item.description: Heineken don announce grand plans to wrap up its nationwide UEFA Champions League campaign with a premium final viewing event for the Arsenal vs PSG match in Lagos. The event go hold on Saturday, May ...

item.title: Jesse Watters Criticized for Personal Attacks While Facing His Own Past Controversies
item.description: Fox News host Jesse Watters faced widespread criticism after personally attacking Texas Democratic Senate candidate Rep. James Talarico for being unmarried. During a live broadcast, Watters questioned...

item.title: U.S. Justice Department subpoenas Reddit and X to identify anonymous users critical of immigration enforcement
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has reportedly issued grand jury subpoenas to Reddit and X (formerly Twitter) as part of criminal investigations aimed at identifying anonymous users who criticized fede...

item.title: Affordable housing development planned for Acadia University area in Nova Scotia
item.description: A new affordable housing initiative is set to bring additional residential units to the Acadia University community in Nova Scotia, according to reporting by CTV News Atlantic. The project is part of ...
